description: Design of outdoor spaces, terrain, water systems, plantings, ecological systems. Where earth meets intention.

dome_relevance.5: Housing — outdoor living environment, yard as extension of home

sphere_relevance.7: Activation — what happens in designed outdoor space
sphere_relevance.8: Permanence — living systems that persist and evolve

adjacent_disciplines.description: Repairing damaged ecosystems, rewilding, native species reintroduction
Water systems, stormwater management, watershed design

practitioners.name: Frederick Law Olmsted
practitioners.practice: Public park design, democratic landscape, nature as public health infrastructure
practitioners.approach: Parks are not decoration — they are public health infrastructure. Central Park was designed so that a factory worker could experience the psychological restoration of nature without leaving the city. Democratic access to landscape beauty.
practitioners.body_of_work: Central Park, Prospect Park, Emerald Necklace (Boston), US Capitol grounds, Biltmore Estate. Designed the first public park system in America. Also reported on slavery in the antebellum South as a journalist.
practitioners.why_instructive: He invented the idea that designed landscape is public health infrastructure — exactly what sphere layer 3 requires. His parks are spheres that have been activating for 150 years.

dome_relevance.4: Parks as public health infrastructure — attention restoration, stress reduction
dome_relevance.8: Community — parks as democratic gathering space
dome_relevance.9: Environment — nature brought into the city

sphere_relevance.1: Parcel design — topography, circulation, spatial sequence
sphere_relevance.3: Environmental — water management, microclimate, ecology within the city
sphere_relevance.5: Social — democratic access regardless of class
sphere_relevance.6: Temporal — parks that have been activating for 150+ years
sphere_relevance.7: Activation — the park experience IS the activation
sphere_relevance.8: Permanence — living systems that grow stronger over decades
sphere_relevance.10: Catalyst — Central Park activated all of Manhattan's development

practitioners.name: Kate Orff
practitioners.practice: Landscape as infrastructure, oyster-tecture, ecological urbanism
practitioners.approach: Landscape is not decoration applied after the building — it IS the infrastructure. Oyster reefs as wave attenuation. Constructed wetlands as water treatment. Living systems that perform engineering functions while providing habitat and beauty.
practitioners.body_of_work: SCAPE Studio founder. Living Breakwaters (Staten Island) — oyster reefs as coastal defense. Lexington Community Farm. Toward an Urban Ecology. MacArthur Fellow 2017.
practitioners.why_instructive: She merges sphere layers 2 (infrastructure) and 3 (environmental) — living systems that do engineering work. This is what sphere activation looks like when it's both beautiful and functional.

dome_relevance.9: Environment — ecological systems that clean and protect

sphere_relevance.7: Activation — ecological infrastructure as public experience
sphere_relevance.8: Permanence — living systems that strengthen over time
sphere_relevance.10: Catalyst — one living infrastructure project inspires others

practitioners.name: Gandalf
practitioners.creator: J.R.R. Tolkien
practitioners.practice: Stewardship, landscape reading, long-term ecological thinking, fireworks
practitioners.approach: The land has memory. Middle-earth's landscapes are not settings — they are characters. The Shire's gardening culture is as important as Gondor's military culture. Gandalf's deepest wisdom is knowing which things must be tended and which must be left alone. He waters no garden that can water itself.
practitioners.body_of_work: Guides the Fellowship through landscapes that are alive with history — Moria, Fangorn, the Dead Marshes, Ithilien. Recognizes that Sauron's fundamental sin is treating land as resource rather than living system. Sam's garden at the end IS the victory.
practitioners.why_instructive: Tolkien's landscapes have temporal depth that sphere layer 6 aspires to. Gandalf's relationship to landscape is stewardship over ownership — he intervenes minimally and lets living systems do their work.

dome_relevance.9: Environment as living entity requiring stewardship, not management
dome_relevance.12: Flourishing — the Shire's flourishing IS the goal

sphere_relevance.3: Environmental — landscape as living system with memory
sphere_relevance.6: Temporal — land carries history, every place is stratified with time
sphere_relevance.8: Permanence — what endures when everything else passes
sphere_relevance.10: Catalyst — one act of restoration (Sam's mallorn tree) transforms a whole place
